description: Fix factual inaccuracies in documentation discovered by auditing docs against source code: incorrect /ws-info endpoint description, incomplete /health response documentation, localhost vs 127.0.0.1 inconsistency, and incorrect ResourceDefinition uri optionality claim.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix /ws-info endpoint description in architecture page
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the architecture page to accurately describe the /ws-info endpoint so that I understand how extension bootstrapping works.
acceptanceCriteria: The /ws-info endpoint description in the HTTP endpoints table no longer claims the endpoint returns the auth secret
The description accurately states the endpoint returns only the WebSocket URL ({ wsUrl })
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx, around line 46.

The current text says /ws-info 'Returns WebSocket URL and auth secret for extension bootstrap'. This is wrong — the endpoint only returns { wsUrl: "ws://..." }. The auth secret is NOT included in the response. The secret is read separately from ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json and sent via the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header during WebSocket upgrade.

Source of truth: platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts, handleWsInfo function (around line 250):
```
const wsUrl = `ws://${url.host}/ws`;
return Response.json({ wsUrl });
```

Fix the description to say something like 'Returns WebSocket URL for extension bootstrap' — remove the 'and auth secret' part. Do NOT change any other endpoint descriptions.

userStories.title: Add missing fields to /health endpoint documentation
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the /health endpoint documentation to list all returned fields so that I can correctly parse the response.
acceptanceCriteria: pluginDetails[].tabs field is documented in the /health response example and key fields list
pluginDetails[].iconSvg field is documented as conditionally present in the /health response
browserToolNames top-level field is documented in the /health response example and key fields list
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx, around lines 140-206 (the /health endpoint section).

Three fields are returned by the code but missing from the docs:

1. pluginDetails[].tabs — an array of { tabId, url, title, ready } objects. Add it after the existing tabState field in the example JSON and key fields list.
2. pluginDetails[].iconSvg — a string containing SVG markup, conditionally included only if the plugin provides an icon. Add a note that this field is optional.
3. browserToolNames — a top-level array of strings listing all registered browser tool names. Add it to the example JSON and key fields list.

Source of truth: platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts, handleHealth function (around lines 305-337). The relevant code:
- tabs: `tabs: tabInfo?.tabs ?? []`
- iconSvg: `...(p.iconSvg ? { iconSvg: p.iconSvg } : {})`
- browserToolNames: `browserToolNames` (top-level field)

Add these fields to both the example JSON response block AND the 'Key fields' description list below it. Match the existing documentation style.

userStories.title: Fix localhost to 127.0.0.1 in troubleshooting page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the troubleshooting page to use the correct host (127.0.0.1) so that I can correctly configure my MCP client.
acceptanceCriteria: Line 103 of troubleshooting.mdx uses http://127.0.0.1:9515/mcp instead of http://localhost:9515/mcp
No other instances of 'localhost' remain in the file (verify with grep)
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx, line 103.

The current text says: 'The endpoint is http://localhost:9515/mcp (not /ws or /)'
It should say: 'The endpoint is http://127.0.0.1:9515/mcp (not /ws or /)'

The codebase standardizes on 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost to avoid IPv6 resolution issues (see platform/shared/src/constants.ts lines 18-20: DEFAULT_HOST = '127.0.0.1' with an explicit comment about why). Every other docs page and CLI command already uses 127.0.0.1. This is the only instance of 'localhost' in the troubleshooting page.

Note: lines 102 and 123 in the same file already correctly use 127.0.0.1, making this inconsistency especially confusing for users.

userStories.title: Fix incorrect uri optionality claim in resources-prompts guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the resources-prompts guide to accurately describe the ResourceDefinition interface so that I define resources correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The narrative text around line 50 no longer claims uri can be omitted
The text accurately states uri is a required string property on every ResourceDefinition
The existing table (which correctly marks uri as required) is not changed
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx, around line 50.

The current narrative text says: 'The `uri` parameter can be omitted when the resource serves a single URI. Include it when your `read()` function needs to dispatch based on the URI value — for example, a resource template that matches multiple URIs and fetches different data depending on which one was requested.'

This is wrong. The TypeScript interface in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts makes uri a required property:
```typescript
export interface ResourceDefinition<TContent extends z.ZodType = z.ZodType> {
  uri: string;  // No ? modifier — required
  ...
}
```

The table at line 67 of the same docs file correctly marks uri as 'Yes' (required), contradicting the narrative text above it.

Rewrite the narrative paragraph to explain that uri is always required. You could say something like: 'The `uri` property uniquely identifies the resource (e.g., `slack://channels`). The `read()` handler receives this URI, which is useful for resource templates that fetch different data based on the URI.'

Do NOT change the table — it's already correct. Only fix the narrative text.
